keyPointsSummary: 'Would have' expresses what would have happened under a different (hypothetical) condition
Often paired with 'If' (third conditional): If I had known, I would have come

quickQuiz.hindi: अगर मुझे पता होता, तो मैं आता।
quickQuiz.answer: If I had known, I would have come.

quickQuiz.hindi: अगर उसने मेहनत की होती, तो वह पास हो जाता।
quickQuiz.answer: If he had worked hard, he would have passed.

quickQuiz.hindi: अगर मेरे पास पैसे होते, तो मैं यह खरीदता।
quickQuiz.answer: If I had money, I would have bought this.

quickQuiz.hindi: अगर तुम मुझे बताते, तो मैं मदद करता।
quickQuiz.answer: If you had told me, I would have helped.

quickQuiz.hindi: अगर वह जल्दी आती, तो हम मिल पाते।
quickQuiz.answer: If she had come early, we would have met.

quickQuiz.hindi: अगर हमें समय मिलता, तो हम जीत जाते।
quickQuiz.answer: If we had time, we would have won.

quickQuiz.hindi: अगर मैं वहाँ होता, तो मैं यह देखता।
quickQuiz.answer: If I had been there, I would have seen this.

quickQuiz.hindi: अगर उन्होंने ध्यान दिया होता, तो वे समझ जाते।
quickQuiz.answer: If they had paid attention, they would have understood.

quickQuiz.hindi: अगर मुझे मौका मिलता, तो मैं यह करता।
quickQuiz.answer: If I had gotten a chance, I would have done this.

quickQuiz.hindi: अगर तुम सुनते, तो तुम सीख जाते।
quickQuiz.answer: If you had listened, you would have learned.

mustRememberRules: 'Would have' expresses what would have happened under a different (hypothetical) condition
Often paired with 'If' (third conditional): If I had known, I would have come
